meta.reasoning: 6c 3h is a very weak offsuit hand. Folding pre-flop against the big blind is the correct play — no reason to invest chips with this holding.
K7o from button position. Marginal hand but position advantage post-flop justifies a call. s4 already folded, leaving SB and BB. Pot odds acceptable at 30:20.
Ace-King suited (Kh Ah) is a premium hand. Raising 3x BB to 60 from the small blind to build the pot and put pressure on remaining active players.

J2 suited is a weak starting hand. Facing a 3x raise from s2 (small blind showing strength) with s1 already in, pot odds (~29%) don't justify continuing with this hand. Folding to preserve chips for better spots.

result.summary: s3 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s3: 1220, s1: 1070, s2: 970, s4: 740). Biggest pot: 1300. Showdowns: 1.
